questions.question: Explain the meaning of a political party.
questions.answer: A political party is an organized group of people who share similar views and work together to contest elections and form the government.

questions.question: Why do political parties contest elections?
questions.answer: Political parties contest elections to gain political power and implement their policies and programmes.

questions.question: How do political parties help in forming government?
questions.answer: The party that wins the majority of seats in elections forms the government and its leaders become ministers.

questions.question: What is meant by public opinion?
questions.answer: Public opinion refers to the collective views and beliefs of people on social and political issues.

questions.question: How do political parties shape public opinion?
questions.answer: They raise important issues, conduct campaigns, and participate in debates to influence people's views.

questions.question: What is a one-party system?
questions.answer: A one-party system is a political system where only one political party is allowed to rule the country.

questions.question: What is a two-party system?
questions.answer: A two-party system is a system in which two major political parties dominate the political scene.

questions.question: Why does India follow a multiparty system?
questions.answer: India follows a multiparty system because it has a diverse society with many interests and opinions.

questions.question: What is meant by national political parties?
questions.answer: National political parties are those that operate across several states and influence national politics.

questions.question: What is meant by state political parties?
questions.answer: State political parties operate mainly within a particular state and focus on regional issues.

questions.question: How do regional parties contribute to democracy?
questions.answer: Regional parties represent the interests and concerns of specific regions in national politics.

questions.question: What is internal democracy in political parties?
questions.answer: Internal democracy means that party members have the right to participate in decision-making and leadership selection.

questions.question: Why is lack of internal democracy considered a challenge?
questions.answer: It concentrates power in a few leaders and reduces participation of ordinary members.

questions.question: Explain dynastic succession in politics.
questions.answer: Dynastic succession occurs when leadership positions in political parties are inherited by family members.

questions.question: Why is money power a challenge in elections?
questions.answer: Money power can influence candidate selection and election outcomes unfairly.

questions.question: What is meant by meaningful choice for voters?
questions.answer: Meaningful choice means voters have different political options with clear policies and programmes.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the anti-defection law?
questions.answer: The anti-defection law prevents elected representatives from changing parties after winning elections.

questions.question: Why is transparency important in elections?
questions.answer: Transparency helps voters know the background and financial details of candidates.

questions.question: How can citizens help improve political parties?
questions.answer: Citizens can demand reforms, participate in politics, and vote responsibly.

questions.question: What role do political parties play in policy making?
questions.answer: Political parties develop policies and implement them when they form the government.

questions.question: How do political parties help citizens access government services?
questions.answer: Local party leaders help people approach government officials and raise their problems.

questions.question: Why are opposition parties important in democracy?
questions.answer: Opposition parties check the government and present alternative policies.

questions.question: How do alliances help political parties?
questions.answer: Alliances increase the chances of winning elections and forming government.

questions.question: Why are political reforms necessary?
questions.answer: Political reforms improve transparency, accountability, and democracy within parties.

questions.question: How do political parties represent social divisions in society?
questions.answer: Different political parties represent the interests of different social groups and ideologies.
